Cost Pricing

Window Cleaning Prices in BC for 2026: What to Expect Across the Province

Published 2026-05-09 · Wagon Windows

Window cleaning prices in BC vary more than most people realize — not just between cities, but within regions, depending on what's actually included in the quote. If you're pricing out service in the Shuswap and comparing quotes you've seen from Vancouver contractors or seen discussed online, the numbers won't translate directly.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Book Online — Free Quote (403) 837-5092

What Window Cleaning Typically Costs Across BC in 2026

Across British Columbia, residential window cleaning for a typical single-family home runs roughly $150–$400 for an exterior-only service, with the range driven by home size, region, and what's included in the scope. Interior-and-exterior combined service runs $250–$600+ for larger or more complex homes. Those ranges are wide because BC's housing stock is wildly diverse: a post-war 900 sq ft bungalow in Prince George is nothing like a 4,000 sq ft new-build in South Surrey. Professional companies price based on time and equipment, and the inputs differ dramatically between a compact rancher and a multi-level home with skylights and specialty glass. Per-window pricing — which some companies advertise — tends to run $8–$15 per window exterior-only in most BC markets. Screens add $3–$6 per screen depending on size. These per-unit prices can seem attractive on simple homes but often miss hidden complexity that a flat-rate quote already accounts for.

  • Lower Mainland (Metro Vancouver): $180–$500+ for residential exterior
  • Okanagan / Interior BC (Kelowna, Vernon): $150–$350 exterior
  • Shuswap region (Salmon Arm, Blind Bay): $169–$299 exterior
  • Northern BC (Prince George, Kamloops area): $130–$280 exterior
  • Screen cleaning province-wide: $3–$6 per screen

These ranges reflect honest, professional service — not the bottom of the market. What you'll pay at the very low end of online quote boards typically reflects incomplete scope, unlicensed operators, or quality that creates the need for a redo.

Lower Mainland vs Interior BC: Why Prices Differ

Window cleaning in Metro Vancouver costs more than in Interior BC for straightforward economic reasons: labour costs, overhead, and competitive market density all price higher in the Lower Mainland. A cleaning company operating in Vancouver is paying more for insurance, fuel, and labour than one operating out of Salmon Arm or Kamloops. But the Lower Mainland's higher baseline price doesn't necessarily mean better quality. Interior BC operators often serve smaller markets where reputation travels further and repeat business matters more. In Salmon Arm, word-of-mouth in a city of 20,000 people is powerful in a way it simply isn't in a metro of 2.5 million. Operators who cut corners don't last. One major difference that pushes Interior BC prices down slightly: simpler housing stock on average. Many Shuswap homes are single-storey or modest two-storey builds without the architectural complexity common in newer Lower Mainland subdivisions. That said, the Shuswap's lake-proximate properties introduce their own complexity — hard water mineral deposits, algae on frames, and hillside lots that require longer ladder setups.

What Shuswap Window Cleaning Prices Actually Include

In the Shuswap market, a legitimate quote from a professional operation should include: cleaning all exterior glass surfaces up to the quoted storey height, squeegee-finishing every pane, and wiping frames and sills dry. Screens are typically priced separately — a good company will tell you this upfront rather than leaving you to discover it later. Wagon Windows includes frame wipe-down with every clean. Tracks are a priced add-on. Screens are $4–5 per screen. Hard water stain removal is quoted separately because severity varies so much: a light haze on a lakeside window is a different job from calcified deposits that have been accumulating for five or six summers. Downspout flushing and condition reporting come with gutter cleaning ($149 for single-storey, $229 for two-storey). These aren't extras — they're part of a complete gutter service because a cleaned gutter with a blocked downspout is still a failed gutter. Knowing what's included means you can make an accurate price comparison. A $120 quote that doesn't include screens or sill wiping is not a better deal than a $169 quote that does — it's a lower number for less work.

Red Flags in Cheap BC Window Cleaning Quotes

Cheap quotes in the BC window cleaning market follow predictable patterns. Knowing the warning signs protects you from paying twice. The biggest red flag is extreme underpricing relative to home size. If a quote for a two-storey, 2,400 sq ft home comes in under $120 for exterior cleaning, something is being cut: scope, time, insurance, or all three. Labour alone for a legitimate two-storey exterior clean on that size home runs well over two hours, which means a sub-$120 price implies either a very low wage or a very fast (incomplete) job. Quotes with vague scope language — 'windows cleaned,' 'full house service' without any specifics — are a signal that the price will expand on arrival. Always ask before booking: does this include screens? What height is covered? Are sills wiped? How are hard water deposits handled? Cash-only operations with no online presence, no reviews, and no verifiable business address warrant caution in any home service category. In window cleaning specifically, working at height creates liability that uninsured operators push onto you as the homeowner if something goes wrong. Unlicensed operations also tend not to carry WCB coverage, which is relevant if a worker is injured on your property. A small price difference is not worth that exposure.

  • Extreme underpricing for home size (under $120 for a full 2-storey exterior)
  • Vague scope that expands on arrival with surprise line items
  • Cash-only, no reviews, no verifiable business address
  • No confirmation of insurance or WCB coverage

Getting a Fair Price for Your Shuswap Home

The cleanest way to get a fair price in the Shuswap is to describe your home accurately when booking and ask explicitly what the quote includes. Size (approximate square footage or number of bedrooms and storeys), whether you want interior service, screen count, and any known hard water issues give a professional company everything needed to quote accurately. For Salmon Arm, Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, and Armstrong properties, Wagon Windows serves the full region and can quote any location in the Shuswap. Prices don't inflate based on distance within the service area — a Blind Bay booking is quoted the same way as a Salmon Arm one. Call (403) 837-5092 or book via the Calendly link at wagonwindow.com. You'll get a confirmed price before the booking is locked — no surprises on invoice day.

Frequently Asked Questions

Are window cleaning prices in Salmon Arm cheaper than in Kelowna or Vancouver?

Generally yes. Interior BC labour and overhead costs are lower than Metro Vancouver, and Kelowna's larger market supports slightly higher pricing than smaller Shuswap communities. That said, the price difference is more moderate than many expect — perhaps 15–30% less than comparable Vancouver service for a standard home.

What's typically not included in a BC window cleaning quote?

Screen cleaning, track cleaning, interior service, and hard water stain removal are commonly excluded from base quotes. Always ask before booking. A professional company will tell you explicitly what's in and out of scope — vague answers are a red flag.

Is there a travel surcharge for properties outside Salmon Arm?

Wagon Windows serves the full Shuswap — Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, Armstrong, and surrounding communities — without a travel surcharge within the service area. Confirm coverage for your specific location when booking.

Why are some online quotes for BC window cleaning under $100?

Sub-$100 quotes for full residential service almost always involve incomplete scope, no insurance, or both. They often don't include screens, second-storey access, or frame cleaning. Paying once for a complete, insured job is better value than paying a low rate for a partial clean — then paying again to fix what was missed.
